violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: the early-return `false` when declares.length === 0
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 94
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: the early-return `false` when declares.length === 0
; misclassifies a literal-only block (no variables, just numeric assertions)
; as non-vacuous. Such a block cannot model any code state and is vacuous,
; yet the function returns false before the counting loop runs.
(declare-const num_declares Int)
(declare-const num_asserts Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
; Code transition: block has no declare-const lines (literal assertions only)
(assert (= num_declares 0))
; Code transition: block has at least one assert line (e.g., (assert (> 5 3)))
(assert (> num_asserts 0))
; Code consequence: early return false fires — loop over declaredNames never runs
(assert (= transition_count 0))
; isVacuous returns false (early return), but with no declared variables,
; no code state is modeled; the block is trivially sat or trivially unsat on literals alone
(assert (= num_declares (- num_asserts num_asserts)))
(check-sat)
; sat — zero-declares path is reachable; isVacuous returns false for a vacuous block
